headline: Oregon seeks upset bid against ranked Eagles in rivalry spot
subhead: The Ducks arrive in Chestnut Hill hungry after dismantling USC, facing a Boston College team that just proved it can win on the road.

xFactor.why: Oregon's quarterback will need to sustain drives and limit mistakes against a Boston College defense that has tightened considerably since early season.

keyMatchup: Oregon's pass rush versus Boston College's offensive line will determine whether the Eagles can control clock and rhythm or whether Oregon forces hurried decisions.
body: Oregon arrived at this moment via a statement victory over USC, a dominant 47-26 performance that reminded the selection committee why the Ducks belong in the conversation. The staff has clearly found rhythm on both sides of the ball, and the confidence that comes with dismantling a ranked opponent translates to road games. Yet Boston College is no cupcake opponent—the Eagles just won on the road at Tennessee, a gritty 34-21 result that showcased both defensive intensity and the ability to execute in hostile environments.

The Eagles sit at 4-3 and ranked sixth for good reason: they win close games and rarely beat themselves. Oregon's challenge is maintaining the tempo and aggression that worked against USC without forcing plays into a Boston College secondary that has steadily improved. The Ducks have the talent to steal this one, but road tests against ranked opponents with solid coaching tend to expose teams that haven't fully jelled. This is the sort of game that defines Oregon's season—a chance to prove last week wasn't a fluke, or a reality check on playoff aspirations.
